data.title: Oil prices little changed ahead of Sino-US trade meeting
data.description:
Oil prices were little changed early on Friday after rising more than 3% in the previous session, as trade tension between top oil consumers U.S. and China showed signs of easing and Britain announced a "breakthrough" trade deal with the United States.

Brent crude rose 7 cents, or 0.1%, to $62.91 a barrel while U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude was up 7 cents, or 0.1%, at $59.98 a barrel as at 0121 GMT. On Thursday, Brent settled up 2.8% at $1.72 and WTI rose 3.2% to $1.84.

U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent will meet China's top economic official Vice Premier He Lifeng in Switzerland on May 10 to work toward resolving trade disputes that have threatened growth in the consumption of crude oil.

Separately, U.S. President Donald Trump and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer announced Britain had agreed to lower tariffs on U.S. imports to 1.8% from 5.1%. The U.S. cut duties on British cars but left a 10% tariff on most other goods.

Elsewhere, the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and allies - or OPEC+ - plan to increase output which could keep pressure on oil prices. A Reuters survey found OPEC oil output edged lower in April as production declines in Libya, Venezuela and Iraq outweighed a scheduled increase in output.

Tighter U.S. sanctions on Iran could restrict supply and push prices higher. Sanctions on two small Chinese refiners for buying Iranian oil made it difficult for them to receive crude and led them to sell their product under alternative names, sources told Reuters on Thursday.

data.title: Uno Minda Earmarks INR2 Billion for New Alloy Wheel Plant in Haryana
data.description:
Uno Minda will invest 2 billion rupees to set up a two-wheeler alloy wheel plant in Bawal, Haryana, according to a Thursday filing on the Indian bourses.

The facility will have a production capacity of 1.5 million units annually and is expected to start operations in Q2 FY27.

The company has also begun production at its expanded Supa, Maharashtra facility, adding 2 million units of capacity.

With the new Bawal plant, Uno Minda's total annual alloy wheel capacity will rise to 9.5 million units.

data.title: Trump fires head of Library of Congress, New York Times reports
data.description:
U.S President Donald Trump's administration has fired the Librarian of Congress, Carla D. Hayden, the New York Times reported on Thursday, citing a spokesperson for the organization.

Hayden, who was the first woman and first African American in the role, headed an office that has overall management responsibility for the library and sets out policy on its programs and activities.

Democratic President Barack Obama appointed her in 2016 to a 10-year term in the role that needed Senate confirmation.

Neither the Library of Congress nor the White House immediately responded to a request for comment from Reuters.

The top Democrat in the U.S. House of Representatives, Hakeem Jeffries, described the move as "unjust".

"There will be accountability for this unprecedented assault on the American way of life sooner rather than later," he said in a statement.

data.title: Australia's Nine Entertainment rises on CoStar buyout of Domain
data.description:
** Shares of Nine Entertainment soar as much as 8.1% to A$1.610, their highest levels since March 26

** Stock logs its biggest intraday pct gain since April 10

** NEC among top gainer in the ASX 200 benchmark

** The media firm intends to vote in favour of U.S. firm CoStar's buyout of Domain Holdings , in which Nine Entertainment holds a 60% stake

** CoStar to buy remaining shares in Domain it does not already own at A$4.43/shr, a 4.2% premium to DHG's last close

** Nine expects to receive A$1.4 billion in cash proceeds, which it intends to distribute as fully franked special dividend

** Special dividend could be in range of 47-49 Au cents per share

** YTD, NEC up 28.3%

data.title: ONE Gas: Offering Expected to Close May 12 >OGS
ONE Gas Prices Public Offering of 2.5M Shares, Expects Gross Proceeds of About $197.5M >OGS
Chambal Fertilisers CFO Steps Down; Successor Named
data.description:
Chambal Fertilisers and Chemicals Chief Financial Officer Anand Agarwal resigned on May 8 to pursue an external opportunity.

He will be relieved from his duties on May 12, according to a Thursday filing on the Indian bourses.

The board has appointed Anuj Jain as CFO effective May 9.

Jain has been with the company since 2001. He has over 38 years of experience across finance, accounts, internal audit, taxation, and secretarial functions.

data.title: Malaysia Faces Growth Risks, Rate Cut Likely Amid Tariff Uncertainty — Market Talk
data.description: Malaysia's export-heavy economy may remain vulnerable to escalating U.S.-China trade tensions, prompting MUFG to cut its 2025 GDP forecast to 4.1% from its previous estimate of 4.7%. Investors have scaled back earlier over-optimism on the ringgit, with further weakening likely amid ongoing macroeconomic challenges, it says. MUFG expects Bank Negara to cut rates by 25 bps this year--possibly in July--bringing the policy rate to 2.75%, as inflation stays benign and dovish rhetoric grows. If the full reciprocal tariff is applied after its 90-day hiatus, it could shave an additional 0.4 ppt from Malaysia's GDP growth, it warns. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

data.title: Lupin Wins US FDA Approval for HIV Drug Raltegravir With 180-Day Market Exclusivity
data.description:
Lupin received approval from the US Food and Drug Administration for its generic version of Raltegravir Tablets USP, 600 mg, the bioequivalent of Isentress HD by Merck, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

As the first-to-file applicant, Lupin is eligible for 180 days of exclusive marketing rights in the US.

The drug, used in combination therapy for HIV-1 treatment, will be manufactured at the company's Nagpur facility.

data.title: Bain Capital to sell China data centre business likely valued at over $4 billion, sources say
data.description:
U.S. investment firm Bain Capital is seeking to sell the China business of data centre operator WinTriX DC Group, in a deal that could value the business at over $4 billion, two people with knowledge of the situation said.

Bain Capital has engaged with advisers who have held preliminary conversations with potential buyers in recent months, said the people, declining to be named as the information was not public.

The China business of WinTriX, formerly known as Chindata Group Holdings, has estimated 2025 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortisation (EBITDA) of close to 4 billion yuan, they said.

Bain Capital declined to comment. The data centre operator did not respond to a Reuters request for comment.

The potential deal comes nearly two years after Bain Capital took Nasdaq-listed Chindata private in a $3.16 billion deal.

Bain Capital first acquired Chinese data centre operator Chindata in 2019 and merged it with Southeast Asia data centre operator Bridge Data Centres in the same year. The listed entity was a combination of both.

Since the take-private, the Boston-based firm separated the two businesses again under WinTriX, said a third person with knowledge of the situation.

The sale also comes as data centre valuations have soared in the last few years, driven by rapid developments of artificial intelligence.

Last year Australian data centre operator AirTrunk was sold to a Blackstone-led consortium for over 20 times its forward core earnings, Reuters reported.

WinTriX's Chinese rival GDS Holdings is currently trading at a price-to-earnings multiple of 8.48 times, LSEG data showed.

Fitch Ratings however in February downgraded WinTriX's long-term foreign- and local-currency issuer default ratings to "BB" from "BBB" with a stable outlook, which it said reflected its expectations that WinTriX would face significantly higher business risks as it changed its strategy to focus on overseas investment.

Fitch cited slower hyperscale data centre demand and higher competition in China as one of the additional risks WinTriX would face.

WinTriX counts social media giant Bytedance as its largest customer, which contributed to 86% of its revenue in 2022, according to the Fitch report.

Outside China, it also operates data centres in India and Malaysia.

In March, WinTriX's unit Bridge Data Centres, which operates data centres outside China, secured a $2.8 billion bank financing to fuel its data centre expansion, it said at the time.

Bain Capital will keep its control of Bridge Data Centres for the time being, said the sources.

data.title: Banpu Target Price Lowered to THB4.80 From THB5.00 by UOB Kay Hian >BANPU.TH
Highfield Resources CEO Steps Down
data.description:
Highfield Resources Chief Executive Officer Ignacio Salazar has resigned, effective immediately, according to a Friday filing with the Australian bourse.

Salazar joined the mining company as CEO in July 2020, per the filing.

data.title: Coastal's Ethanol Unit to Start Commercial Production May 19; Odisha Plant on Hold
data.description:
Coastal said its wholly owned unit Coastal Biotech has completed trial runs for its ethanol project and will begin commercial operations on May 19, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The company expects the ethanol business to boost revenue.

Meanwhile, another subsidiary, Continental Fisheries India, has put its Odisha processing plant project on hold due to geopolitical uncertainties, including proposed US tariffs on Indian exports.

Civil work at the site had begun after land acquisition, but the unit is currently outsourcing shrimp processing to third-party facilities. Coastal said it will reassess the project once market conditions improve.

data.title: Brigade Group to Develop INR20 Billion Commercial Project in Bengaluru
data.description:
BRIGADE ENTERPRISES has acquired an 11-acre plot in Bengaluru to develop a 20 billion Indian rupees commercial project with 1.5 million sq ft of leasable space, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourses.

The company said the Grade A office project will focus on sustainability and support its net zero goals.

data.title: Lost Money on NET Power, Inc.(NPWR)? Join Class Action Suit Seeking Recovery - Contact Levi & Korsinsky
data.description:
NEW YORK, NY / ACCESS Newswire / May 8, 2025 / If you suffered a loss on your NET Power, Inc. investment and want to learn about a potential recovery under the federal securities laws, follow the link below for more information:

https://zlk.com/pslra-1/net-power-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=147663&wire=1

or contact Joseph E. Levi, Esq. via email at jlevi@levikorsinsky.com or call (212) 363-7500 to speak to our team of experienced shareholder advocates.

THE LAWSUIT: A class action securities lawsuit was filed against NET Power, Inc. that seeks to recover losses of shareholders who were adversely affected by alleged securities fraud between June 9, 2023 and March 7, 2025.

CASE DETAILS: The filed complaint alleges that defendants made false statements and/or concealed that: (i) Net Power was unlikely to complete its first utility-scale plant, Project Permian, on schedule and the project was likely to be significantly more expensive than defendants had represented, because of, inter alia, supply chain issues and numerous site- and region-specific challenges; (ii) accordingly, defendants' projections regarding the time and capital needed to complete Project Permian were unrealistic; (iii) the increased time and capital needed to complete Project Permian were likely to have a significant negative impact on the Company's business and financial results; and (iv) as a result, defendants' public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times.

WHAT'S NEXT? If you suffered a loss in NET Power, Inc. stock during the relevant time frame - even if you still hold your shares - go to https://zlk.com/pslra-1/net-power-inc-lawsuit-submission-form?prid=147663&wire=1 to learn about your rights to seek a recovery. There is no cost or obligation to participate.

WHY LEVI & KORSINSKY: Over the past 20 years, Levi & Korsinsky LLP has established itself as a nationally-recognized securities litigation firm that has secured hundreds of millions of dollars for aggrieved shareholders and built a track record of winning high-stakes cases. The firm has extensive expertise representing investors in complex securities litigation and a team of over 70 employees to serve our clients. data.title: Viceroy Hotels Enters Operating Agreement with Marriott
data.description:
Viceroy Hotels has entered into an operating agreement with Marriott Hotels India Private Limited for the management of its hotel property in Hyderabad, according to a Thursday filing on the local bourse.

The hotel, to be operated under the "Courtyard by Marriott" brand, will span approximately 7,000 square yards and offer around 200 guest rooms. The agreement will last until the end of the 30th full fiscal year following the hotel's opening.

This partnership is aimed at enhancing operational standards and aligning with global hospitality practices.

Additionally, the hotel's construction site is owned by the relatives of one of Viceroy's directors, with shareholder approval granted at the company's Extraordinary General Meeting in April 2025.
